page: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with a base speed of 4.5 GHz. The configuration includes 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. Memory capacity is exceptionally high relative to the compute resources, offering 128.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 32.0 GB per vCPU. The server is equipped with 945 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU execution and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier (top 10%), reflecting the high 4.5 GHz clock speed of the AMD EPYC processor. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also achieves top-tier status. Conversely, multi-threaded performance is more constrained due to the low physical core count; the multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmark is categorized in the weak tier (bottom 25%), while multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET operations at 7.7 million operations per second, consistently register in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers high memory capacity per vCPU, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the low core count of 2 physical cores, which limits highly parallelized compute tasks, as demonstrated by the weak multi-threaded decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing benchmarks. This profile is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, single-threaded data processing, and memory-intensive web serving applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical AMD EPYC 9V45 cores running at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 945 GB of local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-threaded performance is limited by the low core count, placing multi-threaded bzip3 decompression in the bottom 25%. This server offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute allocation, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that do not require extensive parallelization. It is suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is an Eadsv7 virtual machine with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine combining high single-core clock speeds with dense RAM and local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and a 4.5 GHz clock speed
128 GB of RAM providing a high 32 GB per vCPU memory density
945 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for in-memory caching, databases, and memory-bound workloads
